report_name: Microsoft Defender Plagued by 'RoguePlanet' Zero-Day Privilege Escalation Vulnerability

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: The 'RoguePlanet' vulnerability is a TOCTOU race condition that is exploited to escalate privileges to NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM. An attacker's script replaces a file with a symbolic link between Defender's time-of-check and time-of-use, causing the privileged Defender service to operate on a protected system file.

attack_patterns.name: Process Injection
attack_patterns.description: After successfully escalating privileges using the 'RoguePlanet' exploit, an attacker can leverage their SYSTEM-level access to perform arbitrary code execution, for example by injecting into other processes.

tools.name: cmd
tools.description: The Windows Command Shell is used to execute the 'mklink' command to create a symbolic link as part of the TOCTOU race condition exploit.

tools.name: fsutil.exe
tools.description: The fsutil.exe utility can be used to create a reparse point (symbolic link) as part of the 'RoguePlanet' exploit chain.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: PowerShell can be used to create a symbolic link via the 'New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink' command as part of the 'RoguePlanet' exploit.

vulnerabilities.name: RoguePlanet
vulnerabilities.description: A time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) race condition vulnerability in Microsoft Defender's file handling logic that allows a local low-privileged user to escalate privileges to SYSTEM by swapping a scanned file with a symbolic link.

identities.name: Microsoft
identities.description: Vendor of the affected products Microsoft Defender, Windows 10, and Windows 11.

identities.name: Nightmare Eclipse
identities.description: The security researcher who publicly disclosed the 'RoguePlanet' vulnerability.

indicators.name: Symbolic Link Creation via Command Line
indicators.pattern:
title: Windows Symbolic Link Creation via Command Line
id: 2b1a3c4d-5e6f-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of symbolic links using built-in Windows command-line utilities 'mklink' or 'fsutil', which can be used for privilege escalation exploits like 'RoguePlanet'.
references:
  - https://securityboulevard.com/2026/06/microsoft-defender-zero-day-privilege-escalation-vulnerability-rogueplanet/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-16
tags:
  - attack.t1068
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_cmd:
    Image|endswith: '\cmd.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'mklink'
  selection_fsutil:
    Image|endswith: '\fsutil.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'reparsepoint'
      - 'set'
  condition: 1 of them
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative or development activity.
indicators.description: Detects the creation of symbolic links using 'mklink' or 'fsutil.exe', a technique used in the 'RoguePlanet' exploit.

indicators.name: Symbolic Link Creation via PowerShell
indicators.pattern:
title: Windows Symbolic Link Creation via PowerShell
id: 8d7c6b5a-4f3e-4d2c-1b0a-9e8d7c6b5a4f
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of symbolic links using PowerShell's New-Item cmdlet, which can be used for privilege escalation exploits like 'RoguePlanet'.
references:
  - https://securityboulevard.com/2026/06/microsoft-defender-zero-day-privilege-escalation-vulnerability-rogueplanet/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-16
tags:
  - attack.t1068
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: 
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'New-Item'
      - '-ItemType'
      - 'SymbolicLink'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative or development activity.
indicators.description: Detects the creation of symbolic links using PowerShell's 'New-Item' cmdlet, a technique mentioned in relation to the 'RoguePlanet' exploit.

relationships.source_name: Symbolic Link Creation via Command Line
